response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals with 17+ years of industry experience and proven expertise in architecture, testing, and high-performance systems, the resume reveals critical gaps for this C++ senior developer role. Most significantly, there is zero professional C++ experience in the past 11 years—the last C++ work was at National Instruments in 2015. All recent positions (2015–present) center on JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, React, and DLang. Additionally, the candidate lacks domain expertise in industrial automation, manufacturing, and industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, Modbus, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, OPC UA), which are core to on-premises automation solutions. The technology and domain shifts are too significant for a mid-senior hands-on developer role requiring immediate C++ productivity.
strengths: Extensive 17+ years of software engineering experience with multiple senior/staff roles demonstrating strong architectural and system design capabilities across diverse domains
Deep focus on software craftsmanship, TDD, and quality practices that align with secure development lifecycle principles and testing requirements
Proven ability to build high-performance, low-latency systems handling millions of requests, showing understanding of scalability, concurrency, and distributed architectures
concerns: No professional C++ work for 11 years (last role: Dec 2014–June 2015); all recent experience is JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, React, DLang, and web technologies
Zero domain expertise in industrial automation, manufacturing solutions, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, secure manufacturing practices, industrial protocols)

Background is web, fintech, and GIS development; no evidence of on-premises solutions, MQTT/IoT protocol experience, or familiarity with industrial communication standards (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, IEC 104, DNP 3.0, OPC UA)

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and IEC 62443 domain knowledge
